text:
American rapper Snoop Dogg will be lighting up during the upcoming 2024 Olympic Games in Paris. Lighting up as in carrying the iconic Olympic torch, of course.

The 52-year-old Snoop Dogg was chosen to hoist the Olympic torch in the final stretch leading up to the opening ceremony Friday night in Paris. Mathieu Hanotin, the mayor of Saint-Denis芒聙聰a suburb of northern Paris that is the home of the Stade de France Olympic Stadium芒聙聰announced the decision on social media.

"Saint Denis: last step before the Eiffel Tower. An international cast [Snoop Dogg] for the last stretch of the Olympic flame," Hanotinsaid in the post, translated to English.

Snoop Dogg confirmed the news on social media Tuesday.

U gots to do it!! 冒聼聭聤冒聼聫驴冒聼聡潞冒聼聡赂冒聼聰楼冒聼聰楼冒聼聫聝冒聼聫驴冒聼楼聡冒聼聡芦冒聼聡路 Will u be watchin??#FollowTheDogg#ParisOlympicspic.twitter.com/3l12Cj74Cp

Joining Snoop Dogg as Olympic torchbearers in Saint-Denis include French actress Laetitia Casta and rapper MC Solaar.

The final torchbearer who will light the Olympic cauldron to begin the opening ceremony has yet to be announced.

title: Snoop Dogg to Carry Olympic Torch in Final Stretch Before Opening Ceremony in Paris
description: The American rapper will play a special role leading up to the Olympic Games.
